session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the integration test patterns for gateway real implementations in this project. Specifically:
1. Are there any integration tests for any `Real*` gateway implementations? Look in `tests/integration/` and `tests/` more broadly. 2. What patterns do they follow? (fixtures, setup, assertions) 3. Is there a test for `fetch_branch` in `RealGitRemoteOps` that I can model a `fetch_prune` test after? 4. Check if there's a `FakeGitRemoteOps` that already has a `fetch_prune` method.
Look at: - tests/integration/ directory structure - Any existing Real gateway integration tests - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/ for the abc and fake - docs/learned/testing/ for testing patterns
session.assistant: I'll help you understand the integration test patterns for gateway real implementations. Let me start by checking the documentation and then exploring the codebase.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)
... [22 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now let me read the relevant testing documentation and examine the existing test patterns:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Integration Testing Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing integration tests that interact with filesystem" 5→ - "testing time-dependent operations" 6→ - "handling mtime resolution in tests" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 8→audit_result: clean 9→--- 10→ 11→# Integration Testing Patterns 12→ 13→Patterns for writing reliable integration tests that interact with real system resources. 14→ 15→## Filesystem Mtime Resolution 16→ 17→Different filesystems have different mtime (modification time) precision: 18→ 19→| Filesystem | Resolution | 20→| ---------- | ---------- | 21→| ext4 | Nanosecond | 22→| HFS+ | 1 second | 23→| APFS | Nanosecond | 24→| FAT32 | 2 seconds | 25→ 26→### Problem: Mtime-Based Tests Flake 27→ 28→When testing mtime-based cache invalidation, operations that complete within the filesystem's resolution may appear unchanged: 29→ 30→```python
... [119 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing tests for gateway fakes that return discriminated unions" 5→ - "deciding whether mutation tracking should occur on error paths" 6→ - "implementing a new fake gateway method with error injection" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a fake gateway without constructor-injected error configuration" 9→ warning: "Fakes must accept error variants at construction time (e.g., push_to_remote_error=PushError(...)) to enable failure injection in tests." 10→ - action: "using truthiness checks or .success on discriminated union results in tests" 11→ warning: "Always use isinstance() for type narrowing. Never check bool(result) or result.success — these bypass the type system." 12→ - action: "tracking mutations before checking error configuration in a fake method" 13→ warning: "Decide deliberately: should this operation track even on failure? push_to_remote skips tracking on error (nothing happened), pull_rebase always tracks (the attempt matters). Match the real operation's semantics." 14→--- 15→ 16→# Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar 17→ 18→This document captures the cross-cutting design decisions in fake gateway testing — specifically, the three patterns that every fake test must exercise and the subtle tracking-on-error decision that differs between operations. 19→ 20→## Why Fakes Need Their Own Tests 21→ 22→Fakes are production code for tests. A broken fake silently produces false-positive test results across the entire test suite. The `tests/unit/fakes/` directory exists to verify that fakes honor their ABC contracts and that their error-injection and mutation-tracking machinery works correctly. 23→ 24→<!-- Source: tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py --> 25→ 26→See `test_fake_git_remote_ops.py` in `tests/unit/fakes/` for the canonical exemplar. 27→ 28→## Three Required Test Categories Per Operation 29→ 30→Every fake method that returns a discriminated union needs three test categories:
... [66 lines omitted] ...
35→| **Error injection** | Constructor-configured error is returned | Proves failure paths work without modifying fake internals | 40→## The Tracking-on-Error Decision 42→The most subtle design choice in fake implementation is whether mutation tracking should occur when an error is configured. This is not arbitrary — it follows the real operation's semantics: 44→| Operation | Tracks on error? | Rationale | 52→Compare `FakeGitRemoteOps.push_to_remote()` (early-returns error before tracking) with `FakeGitRemoteOps.pull_rebase()` (tracks first, then checks error) in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py`. 54→**When implementing a new fake method**, ask: "If the real operation fails, did a side effect still occur?" If yes, track before checking the error. If no, check the error first and skip tracking. 81→if isinstance(result, PushError): 85→**Testing only the happy path:** Every discriminated union method needs both success and error tests. A fake that only tests success could silently break error injection for all consumers. 90→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) — Design decisions for when to use unions vs exceptions

1→"""Integration tests for RealGitBranchOps. 2→ 3→Tests verify that RealGitBranchOps correctly executes git branch operations 4→using actual git subprocess calls. 5→""" 6→ 7→import subprocess 8→from pathlib import Path 9→ 10→import pytest 11→ 12→from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.real import RealGitBranchOps 13→from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 14→from tests.integration.conftest import GitBranchOpsSetup, init_git_repo 15→ 16→ 17→def test_create_branch_creates_new_branch(git_branch_ops: GitBranchOpsSetup) -> None: 18→ """Test that create_branch creates a new branch from the specified start point.""" 19→ branch_ops, git, repo = git_branch_ops 20→ 21→ # Act 22→ branch_ops.create_branch(repo, "feature-branch", "main", force=False) 23→ 24→ # Assert 25→ branches = git.branch.list_local_branches(repo) 26→ assert "feature-branch" in branches 27→ 28→ 29→def test_create_branch_from_specific_commit(git_branch_ops: GitBranchOpsSetup) -> None: 30→ """Test that create_branch can create a branch from a specific commit."""
... [274 lines omitted] ...
80→ # Act & Assert: Should raise error for unmerged branch 81→ with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="delete branch"): 201→ # Assert: No error was raised, operation succeeded

1→"""Abstract base class for Git remote operations. 2→ 3→This sub-gateway extracts remote operations from the main Git gateway, 4→including fetch, pull, and push operations. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 10→from pathlib import Path 11→ 12→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import ( 13→ PullRebaseError, 14→ PullRebaseResult, 15→ PushError, 16→ PushResult, 17→) 18→ 19→ 20→class GitRemoteOps(ABC): 21→ """Abstract interface for Git remote operations. 22→ 23→ This interface contains both mutation and query operations for remotes. 24→ All implementations (real, fake, dry-run, printing) must implement this interface. 25→ """ 26→ 27→ @abstractmethod 28→ def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: 29→ """Fetch from a remote and prune deleted tracking branches. 30→
... [123 lines omitted] ...
81→ subprocess.CalledProcessError: If git command fails 94→ ) -> PushResult | PushError: 105→ PushResult on success, PushError on failure. 112→ ) -> PullRebaseResult | PullRebaseError: 125→ PullRebaseResult on success, PullRebaseError on failure. 145→ ValueError: If remote doesn't exist or has no URL

1→"""Fake implementation of Git remote operations for testing.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from pathlib import Path 6→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 9→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import ( 10→ PullRebaseError, 11→ PullRebaseResult, 12→ PushError, 13→ PushResult, 14→) 15→ 16→if TYPE_CHECKING: 17→ # Import PushedBranch type from parent module to avoid circular import 18→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import PushedBranch 19→ 20→ 21→class FakeGitRemoteOps(GitRemoteOps): 22→ """In-memory fake implementation of Git remote operations. 23→ 24→ This fake accepts pre-configured state in its constructor and tracks 25→ mutations for test assertions. 26→ 27→ Constructor Injection: 28→ --------------------- 29→ - remote_urls: Mapping of (repo_root, remote_name) -> remote URL 30→ - pull_branch_raises: Exception to raise when pull_branch() is called
... [200 lines omitted] ...
31→ - push_to_remote_error: PushError to return when push_to_remote() is called 32→ - pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError to return when pull_rebase() is called 48→ fetch_branch_raises: Exception | None = None, 49→ pull_branch_raises: Exception | None = None, 50→ push_to_remote_error: PushError | None = None, 51→ pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError | None = None, 52→ fetch_prune_raises: Exception | None = None, 58→ fetch_branch_raises: Exception to raise when fetch_branch() is called 59→ pull_branch_raises: Exception to raise when pull_branch() is called 60→ push_to_remote_error: PushError to return when push_to_remote() is called 61→ pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError to return when pull_rebase() is called 67→ self._push_to_remote_error = push_to_remote_error 68→ self._pull_rebase_error = pull_rebase_error 117→ ) -> PushResult | PushError: 118→ """Record push to remote, or return error if failure configured.""" 122→ if self._push_to_remote_error is not None: 123→ return self._push_to_remote_error 131→ ) -> PullRebaseResult | PullRebaseError: 134→ Tracks call for test assertions. Returns configured error if set. 137→ if self._pull_rebase_error is not None: 138→ return self._pull_rebase_error 145→ ValueError: If remote doesn't exist or has no URL 149→ raise ValueError(f"Remote '{remote}' not found in repository")

1→"""Production implementation of Git remote operations using subprocess.""" 2→ 3→import subprocess 4→from pathlib import Path 5→ 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.lock import wait_for_index_lock 7→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 8→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import ( 9→ PullRebaseError, 10→ PullRebaseResult, 11→ PushError, 12→ PushResult, 13→) 14→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time 15→from erk_shared.subprocess_utils import copied_env_for_git_subprocess, run_subprocess_with_context 16→ 17→# Timeout in seconds for network-touching git operations (push, pull, fetch). 18→# Prevents indefinite hangs on network issues or credential prompts. 19→_GIT_NETWORK_TIMEOUT = 120 20→ 21→ 22→class RealGitRemoteOps(GitRemoteOps): 23→ """Real implementation of Git remote operations using subprocess.""" 24→ 25→ def __init__(self, time: Time) -> None: 26→ """Initialize RealGitRemoteOps with Time provider. 27→ 28→ Args: 29→ time: Time provider for lock waiting 30→ """

1→"""Tests for FakeGitRemoteOps.""" 2→ 3→from pathlib import Path 4→ 5→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import PushedBranch 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.fake import FakeGitRemoteOps 7→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import ( 8→ PullRebaseError, 9→ PullRebaseResult, 10→ PushError, 11→ PushResult, 12→) 13→ 14→ 15→class TestPushToRemote: 16→ def test_returns_push_result_on_success(self) -> None: 17→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps() 18→ result = ops.push_to_remote( 19→ Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False 20→ ) 21→ assert isinstance(result, PushResult) 22→ 23→ def test_returns_push_error_when_configured(self) -> None: 24→ error = PushError(message="rejected") 25→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=error) 26→ result = ops.push_to_remote( 27→ Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False 28→ ) 29→ assert isinstance(result, PushError) 30→ assert result.message == "rejected"
... [44 lines omitted] ...
39→ def test_does_not_track_pushed_branch_on_error(self) -> None: 40→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(message="rejected")) 51→ def test_returns_pull_rebase_error_when_configured(self) -> None: 52→ error = PullRebaseError(message="conflict") 53→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(pull_rebase_error=error) 55→ assert isinstance(result, PullRebaseError) 64→ def test_tracks_pull_rebase_call_even_on_error(self) -> None: 66→ ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(pull_rebase_error=PullRebaseError(message="conflict"))

1→"""Integration tests for git operations. 2→ 3→These tests verify that RealGit correctly handles git operations with real git repositories. 4→Integration tests use actual git subprocess calls to validate the abstractions. 5→""" 6→ 7→import subprocess 8→from pathlib import Path 9→ 10→import pytest 11→ 12→from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 13→from tests.integration.conftest import ( 14→ GitSetup, 15→ GitWithDetached, 16→ GitWithExistingBranch, 17→ GitWithWorktrees, 18→ init_git_repo, 19→) 20→ 21→ 22→def test_list_worktrees_single_repo(git_ops: GitSetup) -> None: 23→ """Test listing worktrees returns only main repository when no worktrees exist.""" 24→ worktrees = git_ops.git.worktree.list_worktrees(git_ops.repo) 25→ 26→ assert len(worktrees) == 1 27→ assert worktrees[0].path == git_ops.repo 28→ assert worktrees[0].branch == "main" 29→ 30→

1→"""Unit tests for RealGit with mocked subprocess calls. 2→ 3→These tests verify that RealGit correctly constructs subprocess commands 4→for external tools (git) without actually executing them. 5→""" 6→ 7→from pathlib import Path 8→from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit 11→ 12→ 13→def test_list_remote_branches() -> None: 14→ """Test git branch -r with mocked subprocess.""" 15→ with patch("subprocess.run") as mock_run: 16→ # Arrange: Configure mock to return sample remote branches 17→ mock_run.return_value = MagicMock( 18→ stdout="origin/main
origin/feature-1
origin/feature-2
", 19→ returncode=0, 20→ ) 21→ 22→ # Act: Call the method 23→ ops = RealGit() 24→ branches = ops.branch.list_remote_branches(Path("/test/repo")) 25→ 26→ # Assert: Verify command construction 27→ mock_run.assert_called_once() 28→ call_args = mock_run.call_args 29→ assert call_args[0][0] == ["git", "branch", "-r", "--format=%(refname:short)"] 30→ assert call_args[1]["cwd"] == Path("/test/repo")

1→"""Integration tests for RealGitCommitOps.commit_files_to_branch(). 2→ 3→Tests verify that commit_files_to_branch correctly creates commits on a branch 4→using git plumbing commands without modifying the working tree or HEAD. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→import subprocess 10→from pathlib import Path 11→ 12→import pytest 13→ 14→from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.real import RealGitCommitOps 15→from erk_shared.gateway.time.real import RealTime 16→from tests.integration.conftest import init_git_repo 17→ 18→ 19→def _make_commit_ops() -> RealGitCommitOps: 20→ return RealGitCommitOps(time=RealTime()) 21→ 22→ 23→def test_commit_files_to_branch_creates_commit_on_target_branch(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 24→ """Test that commit_files_to_branch creates a commit on the target branch.""" 25→ repo = tmp_path / "repo" 26→ repo.mkdir() 27→ init_git_repo(repo, "main") 28→ 29→ commit_ops = _make_commit_ops() 30→

session.assistant: Perfect! Now let me check the doc on architecture patterns for gateways:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-15 18:50 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding or modifying methods in any gateway ABC interface (Git, GitHub, Graphite)" 7→ - "implementing new gateway operations" 8→ - "composing one gateway inside another (e.g., GitHub composing GitHubIssues)" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "adding a new method to Git ABC" 11→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 12→ - action: "adding a new method to GitHub ABC" 13→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 14→ - action: "adding a new method to Graphite ABC" 15→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 16→ - action: "removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC" 17→ warning: "Must remove from 5 places simultaneously: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py. Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites to use subgateway property. Verify with grep across packages." 18→ - action: "adding subprocess.run or run_subprocess_with_context calls to a gateway real.py file" 19→ warning: "Must add integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_*.py. Real gateway methods with subprocess calls need tests that verify the actual subprocess behavior." 20→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(|run_subprocess_with_context\\(" 21→ - action: "making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data" 22→ warning: "Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern." 23→ - action: "using subprocess.run with git command outside of a gateway" 24→ warning: "Use the Git gateway instead. Direct subprocess calls bypass testability (fakes) and dry-run support. The Git ABC (erk_shared.gateway.git.abc.Git) likely already has a method for this operation. Only use subprocess directly in real.py gateway implementations." 25→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(\\s*\\[.*[\"']git" 26→ - action: "changing gateway return type to discriminated union" 27→ warning: "Verify all 5 implementations import the new types. Missing imports in abc.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, or printing.py break the gateway pattern." 28→ - action: "designing error handling for a new gateway method" 29→ warning: "Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section." 30→ - action: "adding a new parameter to a gateway ABC method"
... [772 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "All 5 implementations must be updated (ABC, Real, Fake, DryRun, Printing). Fake may accept but not track new parameters when assertion is not needed for tests." 42→**Exception: erk-dev** (`packages/erk-dev/`) is developer tooling and is exempt from these rules. Direct `subprocess.run` with git commands is acceptable in erk-dev since it doesn't need gateway abstractions for its own operations. 136→When designing error handling for a new gateway method, the key question is: **does the caller continue after the failure?** 138→This checklist helps you choose between discriminated unions and exceptions. For full pattern documentation, see [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md). 140→### Decision: Discriminated Union or Exception? 144→| Does at least one caller branch on the error and continue with different logic? | Discriminated union | Exception | 145→| Does the error carry domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message`? | Discriminated union | Exception | 146→| Do all callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop)? | Exception | Discriminated union | 150→- [ ] At least one caller branches on the error and continues with different logic 151→- [ ] Error type carries domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message` 152→- [ ] Error type implements the `NonIdealState` protocol (`error_type` + `message` properties) from `erk_shared.non_ideal_state` 156→### Checklist: Use Exceptions When 158→- [ ] All callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop) 159→- [ ] No caller inspects error type or branches on error content 160→- [ ] Use `UserFacingCliError` at CLI boundary 166→All discriminated union error types must implement the `NonIdealState` protocol from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`. The protocol requires two read-only properties: `error_type` (machine-readable classification string) and `message` (human-readable description). 181→**Methods using discriminated unions** (callers branch on error): 185→| `merge_pr` | `MergeResult \| MergeError` | GitHub | 186→| `push_to_remote` | `PushResult \| PushError` | Git remote_ops | 187→| `pull_rebase` | `PullRebaseResult \| PullRebaseError` | Git remote_ops | 189→| `submit_branch` | `SubmitBranchResult \| SubmitBranchError` | BranchManager | 191→**Methods using exceptions** (all callers terminate): 193→| Method | Exception | Why | 195→| Worktree add/remove | `RuntimeError` | No caller inspects error content; all terminate identically | 196→| HTTP operations | `HttpError` | Generic transport failure; callers just surface the message | 200→When changing an existing gateway method's return type (e.g., converting from exception-based to discriminated union), follow this comprehensive migration pattern: 207→ - [ ] `real.py` - Return appropriate error types for failure cases 209→ - [ ] `dry_run.py` - Return appropriate success/error based on mode 212→5. [ ] Update tests to check `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 217→**PR #6294** (`merge_pr: bool | str` → `MergeResult | MergeError`): 221→- Updated tests to use `isinstance(result, MergeError)` 243→Some resources benefit from existence-check methods that enable Look Before You Leap validation. This pattern prevents cryptic errors when fetching non-existent resources. 251→- When error messages from `get_X()` on missing resources are unclear 274→ user_output(f"Error: Issue #{issue_number} not found") 353→5. **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Update any tests that assumed failure on missing resource 597→The worktree sub-gateway follows the same 5-file pattern with methods: `list_worktrees()`, `add_worktree()`, `move_worktree()`, `remove_worktree()`, `prune_worktrees()`, `find_worktree_for_branch()`. Note: Worktree operations use exceptions (RuntimeError), not discriminated unions. 686→**Partial removal causes type checker errors** - if you remove from abc.py but forget printing.py, the type checker will complain that PrintingGit doesn't implement the abstract method. 704→1. Run type checker (`ty`) to verify no abstract method errors 761→├── abc.py # push_to_remote() -> PushResult | PushError 763→├── fake.py # Constructor-injected errors, mutation tracking 770→- **Discriminated union returns**: Methods return `Success | Error` instead of raising 771→- **Error boundary in real.py**: `try/except RuntimeError` converts to `PushError` 772→- **Fake configuration**: `FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(...))` injects failures 795→- [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) - Where exceptions become discriminated unions
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report on the integration test patterns for gateway real implementations:
## Summary Report: Integration Test Patterns for Gateway Real Implementations
Based on my thorough exploration of the erk codebase, here's what I found:
### 1. **Are there integration tests for Real* gateway implementations?**
Yes. The project maintains **two distinct test directories** for Real implementations:
- **`tests/real/`** - Tests for Real* implementations that mock subprocess calls (Layer 2) - **`tests/integration/`** - Tests for Real* implementations with actual git/filesystem I/O (Layer 2b)
The key distinction: - `tests/real/` mocks subprocess calls using `@patch` to verify command construction and output parsing - `tests/integration/` calls real git commands in `tmp_path` directories to validate actual behavior
### 2. **What patterns do they follow?**
#### Integration Test Pattern (tests/integration/)
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/test_real_git_branch_ops.py`
```python def test_create_branch_creates_new_branch(git_branch_ops: GitBranchOpsSetup) -> None: """Test that create_branch creates a new branch from the specified start point.""" branch_ops, git, repo = git_branch_ops
# Act branch_ops.create_branch(repo, "feature-branch", "main", force=False)
# Assert branches = git.branch.list_local_branches(repo) assert "feature-branch" in branches ```
**Key characteristics:** - Uses **fixtures** from `tests/integration/conftest.py` that set up real git repos - `GitBranchOpsSetup` namedtuple provides: `(branch_ops, git, repo)` where repo is a real tmp_path directory - Calls **actual git commands** via subprocess within fixture setup (`init_git_repo`, `git worktree add`, etc.) - Verifies behavior through real git queries - No mocking of subprocess
#### Real Implementation Test Pattern (tests/real/)
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/real/test_real_git.py`
```python def test_list_remote_branches() -> None: """Test git branch -r with mocked subprocess.""" with patch("subprocess.run") as mock_run: # Arrange: Configure mock to return sample remote branches mock_run.return_value = MagicMock( stdout="origin/main\norigin/feature-1\norigin/feature-2\n", returncode=0, )
# Act: Call the method ops = RealGit() branches = ops.branch.list_remote_branches(Path("/test/repo"))
# Assert: Verify command construction mock_run.assert_called_once() call_args = mock_run.call_args assert call_args[0][0] == ["git", "branch", "-r", "--format=%(refname:short)"] ```
**Key characteristics:** - Mocks subprocess with `@patch` - Verifies **command construction** (the exact command arguments) - Verifies **output parsing** (handling of stdout) - Fast, no real I/O - Tests command-building logic, not external tool behavior
### 3. **Is there a test for `fetch_branch` to model `fetch_prune` after?**
Yes! The fake has extensive tests in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py`:
```python class TestPushToRemote: def test_returns_push_result_on_success(self) -> None: ops = FakeGitRemoteOps() result = ops.push_to_remote( Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False ) assert isinstance(result, PushResult)
def test_returns_push_error_when_configured(self) -> None: error = PushError(message="rejected") ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=error) result = ops.push_to_remote( Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False ) assert isinstance(result, PushError) assert result.message == "rejected"
def test_tracks_pushed_branch_on_success(self) -> None: ops = FakeGitRemoteOps() ops.push_to_remote(Path("/repo"), "origin", "feature", set_upstream=True, force=False) assert ops.pushed_branches == [ PushedBranch(remote="origin", branch="feature", set_upstream=True, force=False) ]
def test_does_not_track_pushed_branch_on_error(self) -> None: ops = FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(message="rejected")) ops.push_to_remote(Path("/repo"), "origin", "main", set_upstream=False, force=False) assert ops.pushed_branches == [] ```
**For fetch_branch**, I found references in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_reconcile.py` using it, but **no direct fake tests for fetch_branch yet** (only `fetch_prune_calls`, `fetched_branches`, `pulled_branches`, `pushed_branches`, `pull_rebase_calls` are tracked).
### 4. **Does FakeGitRemoteOps have fetch_prune already?**
Yes! **Both the ABC and Fake already have `fetch_prune` implemented:**
**ABC** (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/abc.py`): ```python @abstractmethod def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: """Fetch from a remote and prune deleted tracking branches. Runs `git fetch --prune <remote>` to update remote refs and remove any remote-tracking references that no longer exist on the remote. """ ... ```
**Fake** (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py`): ```python def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: """Fetch and prune from remote (tracks mutation).""" if self._fetch_prune_raises is not None: raise self._fetch_prune_raises self._fetch_prune_calls.append((repo_root, remote))
@property def fetch_prune_calls(self) -> list[tuple[Path, str]]: """Read-only access to fetch_prune calls for test assertions. Returns list of (repo_root, remote) tuples. """ return list(self._fetch_prune_calls) ```
**Real** (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/real.py`): ```python def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: """Fetch from remote and prune deleted tracking branches.""" run_subprocess_with_context( cmd=["git", "fetch", "--prune", remote], operation_context=f"fetch --prune from remote '{remote}'", cwd=repo_root, timeout=_GIT_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, env=copied_env_for_git_subprocess(), ) ```
### Key Testing Architecture Layers
Based on `tests/AGENTS.md` and examined files:
``` Layer 1: tests/unit/fakes/ - Tests OF fakes themselves └─ test_fake_git_remote_ops.py verifies fake behavior
Layer 2: tests/real/ - Real* mocked subprocess tests └─ Command construction & output parsing verified └─ Example: test_list_remote_branches
Layer 2b: tests/integration/ - Real implementation integration tests └─ Actual git subprocess calls in tmp_path └─ Examples: test_real_git_branch_ops.py, test_real_git_commit_ops.py
Layer 3: tests/commands/ - CLI tests using fakes
Layer 4: tests/core/ - Business logic tests using fakes ```
### Fixture Setup Pattern (for Integration Tests)
**From `tests/integration/conftest.py`:**
```python class GitBranchOpsSetup(NamedTuple): branch_ops: GitBranchOps git: Git repo: Path
@pytest.fixture def git_branch_ops(tmp_path: Path) -> Iterator[GitBranchOpsSetup]: """Provide RealGitBranchOps with setup repo for integration testing.""" repo = tmp_path / "repo" repo.mkdir() init_git_repo(repo, "main") yield GitBranchOpsSetup(branch_ops=RealGitBranchOps(), git=RealGit(), repo=repo)
def init_git_repo(repo_path: Path, default_branch: str = "main") -> None: """Initialize a git repository with initial commit.""" subprocess.run(["git", "init", "-b", default_branch], cwd=repo_path, check=True) subprocess.run(["git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"], cwd=repo_path, check=True) subprocess.run(["git", "config", "user.name", "Test User"], cwd=repo_path, check=True)
test_file = repo_path / "README.md" test_file.write_text("# Test Repository\n", encoding="utf-8") subprocess.run(["git", "add", "README.md"], cwd=repo_path, check=True) subprocess.run(["git", "commit", "-m", "Initial commit"], cwd=repo_path, check=True) ```
### Critical Testing Patterns from docs/learned/
**From `gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md`:** - Every discriminated union method needs **3 test categories**: 1. Default success (no-arg construction) 2. Error injection (configured error) 3. Mutation tracking (for write methods)
- **Tracking-on-error decision**: - `push_to_remote`: **Does NOT track on error** (nothing happened, so don't record it) - `pull_rebase`: **DOES track on error** (the attempt was made, record it)
**From `integration-testing-patterns.md`:** - Use `time.sleep(0.01)` (10ms) between operations if testing mtime-based cache invalidation - Use `tmp_path` fixture for real directories - Set timeout on subprocess calls (e.g., `timeout=30` for network operations)
### Absolute File Paths for Reference
1. **ABC Definition**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/abc.py` 2. **Real Implementation**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/real.py` 3. **Fake Implementation**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py` 4. **Fake Tests**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_git_remote_ops.py` 5. **Integration Test Fixtures**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/conftest.py` 6. **Integration Test Example**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/test_real_git_branch_ops.py` 7. **Integration Test Example (commit_ops)**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/test_real_git_commit_ops.py` 8. **Real Test Example**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/real/test_real_git.py` 9. **Testing Docs**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md` 10. **Testing Docs**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/integration-testing-patterns.md` 11. **Gateway Architecture**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md`
